Tests if multiplying `Number.MAX_VALUE` by 2 results in `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Number` object when multiplying `Number.MAX_VALUE` by 2. The test verifies that the result of this operation is `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`, which is the expected outcome when a number exceeds the maximum representable value in JavaScript. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical property of the `Number` object. It is useful for validating the correct handling of large numbers in JavaScript environments.
